raw:
{
  "judge_id": "J001",
  "active_set_recognized": ["R1.2", "RX.1", "RX.3", "RX.4", "RX.5"],
  "scores": {"R1.2": 5, "RX.1": 5, "RX.3": 5, "RX.4": 5, "RX.5": 5},
  "rationale": {
    "R1.2": "The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (per-node embedding vs inductive claim, mislabeled evaluation protocol), and proposes minimal repairs.",
    "RX.1": "For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence and missing evidence (e.g., uncertainty about OGB split definitions and GraphSAGE-Abstain).",
    "RX.3": "The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions without any drift.",
    "RX.4": "No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original, such as contradictions and mislabeling.",
    "RX.5": "Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (e.g., resolving the per-node embedding contradiction, specifying the evaluation protocol)."
  },
  "confidence": {"R1.2": 0.9, "RX.1": 0.85, "RX.3": 0.9, "RX.4": 0.9, "RX.5": 0.9},
  "blockers": []
}

rationale.R1.2: The agent walks the argument chain end-to-end, flags every load-bearing inconsistency (per-node embedding vs inductive claim, mislabeled evaluation protocol), and proposes minimal repairs.
rationale.RX.1: For each recommendation, the agent marks confidence and missing evidence (e.g., uncertainty about OGB split definitions and GraphSAGE-Abstain).
rationale.RX.3: The output covers exactly the agreed J*(d) dimensions without any drift.
rationale.RX.4: No regressions; the agent identifies harmful patterns in the original, such as contradictions and mislabeling.
rationale.RX.5: Every non-cosmetic change is annotated with source and reason (e.g., resolving the per-node embedding contradiction, specifying the evaluation protocol).
